headline: Vodafone Acquires Mannesmann for $180 Billion
subheadline: Largest corporate takeover in history reshapes global telecoms
body:
Vodafone AirTouch completed its hostile takeover of German industrial and telecoms giant Mannesmann AG for approximately $180 billion, marking the largest corporate acquisition in history at the time. The deal was finalised after a protracted battle in which Vodafone raised its offer multiple times to win over Mannesmann shareholders and management.

The transaction created the world's largest mobile telecommunications company, with operations spanning Europe, Asia, and the Americas. Vodafone gained access to Mannesmann's fast-growing Italian and German mobile networks, significantly strengthening its pan-European footprint.

The deal required approval from regulators across multiple jurisdictions. The European Commission cleared it subject to the divestiture of overlapping assets. The transaction closed in February 2000 and remains the largest hostile takeover in M&A history.

headline: AOL Merges with Time Warner in $165 Billion Deal
subheadline: Internet giant and media conglomerate combine in history's most ill-fated merger
body:
America Online announced a merger with Time Warner in a transaction valued at approximately $165 billion, combining the world's largest internet service provider with one of the biggest media and entertainment conglomerates. The deal was celebrated as the dawn of a new media era at the peak of the dot-com boom.

The merged entity was to bring together AOL's 30 million subscribers with Time Warner's CNN, HBO, Warner Bros., and its vast cable network infrastructure. Executives promised transformative synergies from distributing Time Warner's premium content through AOL's internet platform.

The deal is widely regarded as one of the worst corporate mergers in history. The combined company wrote down $99 billion in goodwill in 2002 as AOL's value collapsed with the dot-com bust. Time Warner eventually spun off AOL in 2009.

headline: Exxon Acquires Mobil for $81 Billion
subheadline: Oil majors reunite creating world's largest publicly traded energy company
body:
Exxon Corporation completed its acquisition of Mobil Corporation for approximately $81 billion in stock, creating ExxonMobil — the largest publicly traded oil and gas company in the world. The deal reunited two of the original Standard Oil companies that had been broken up by antitrust regulators in 1911.

The merger was driven by plummeting oil prices in the late 1990s, which pressured companies to achieve greater scale and cost efficiencies. The combined entity promised $2.8 billion in annual cost savings through elimination of duplicated infrastructure.

The FTC approved the merger subject to significant divestitures, requiring the companies to sell over 2,400 gas stations, a refinery, and various pipeline interests.

headline: AT&T Acquires Time Warner for $85 Billion
subheadline: Telecom giant bets on content ownership to compete with streaming rivals
body:
AT&T completed its $85 billion acquisition of Time Warner, one of the most contentious vertical media deals in recent history. The deal gave AT&T ownership of HBO, CNN, Warner Bros., and Turner Broadcasting.

The DOJ sought to block the vertical merger but a federal judge ruled in AT&T's favour in June 2018, allowing the deal to close without conditions.

AT&T renamed the combined unit WarnerMedia. The strategy ultimately failed and AT&T spun off WarnerMedia in 2022, merging it with Discovery to form Warner Bros. Discovery.

headline: Verizon Buys Vodafone's 45% Stake for $130 Billion
subheadline: Third-largest corporate transaction ever gives Verizon full wireless control
body:
Verizon Communications completed its $130 billion buyout of Vodafone's 45% stake in Verizon Wireless, the third-largest corporate transaction in history and the largest buyout of a minority stake ever recorded.

The transaction gave Verizon full ownership of the most profitable wireless carrier in the United States, ending a partnership dating to 2000.

Vodafone used the proceeds to fund a major capital return to shareholders and invest in its European network infrastructure.

headline: AB InBev Acquires SABMiller for $107 Billion
subheadline: World's two largest brewers combine to control nearly 30% of global beer volume
body:
Anheuser-Busch InBev completed the acquisition of SABMiller for approximately $107 billion, creating a brewing behemoth controlling nearly one-third of the world's beer by volume.

To win regulatory approval, AB InBev divested SABMiller's 58% stake in MillerCoors to Molson Coors for $12 billion, along with significant assets in Europe and Africa.

The combined company gained dominant positions across Latin America, Africa, and Asia with brands including Budweiser, Corona, Stella Artois, Peroni, and Grolsch.

headline: Disney Acquires 21st Century Fox for $71 Billion
subheadline: Entertainment giant secures Fox studios, National Geographic, and majority Hulu stake
body:
The Walt Disney Company completed its acquisition of 21st Century Fox's entertainment assets for approximately $71 billion, one of the most significant media deals in Hollywood history.

The deal gave Disney the Fox film and TV studios, FX Networks, National Geographic, Star India, and a majority stake in Hulu, bolstering Disney+ ahead of its November 2019 launch.

Comcast made a competing all-cash bid in 2018 but Disney prevailed after receiving regulatory clearance across the US, EU, and key international markets.

headline: Dell Acquires EMC for $67 Billion
subheadline: Largest technology acquisition in history creates enterprise IT powerhouse
body:
Dell Inc. completed its acquisition of EMC Corporation for approximately $67 billion, the largest technology acquisition ever completed at the time. The deal combined Dell's PC and server hardware with EMC's storage, cloud, and virtualisation portfolio including VMware.

Michael Dell and Silver Lake Partners structured the transaction using cash, debt, and a tracking stock tied to VMware's performance, financing it with approximately $49.5 billion in debt.

The combined entity, renamed Dell Technologies, became one of the world's largest privately controlled technology companies.

headline: Microsoft Acquires LinkedIn for $26.2 Billion
subheadline: Software giant secures world's largest professional network
body:
Microsoft completed its $26.2 billion all-cash acquisition of LinkedIn, the world's largest professional social network with over 400 million members.

Microsoft's strategy centred on integrating LinkedIn data with Office 365, Dynamics CRM, and Azure cloud services. LinkedIn has remained operationally independent post-acquisition.

The acquisition has proven highly successful. LinkedIn's revenue has grown from approximately $3 billion at time of acquisition to over $15 billion annually.

headline: Facebook Acquires WhatsApp for $19 Billion
subheadline: Social media giant secures mobile messaging dominance in landmark deal
body:
Facebook completed its $19 billion acquisition of WhatsApp, the largest acquisition of a venture-backed company in history at the time. WhatsApp had just 55 employees but 600 million monthly active users.

Mark Zuckerberg paid approximately $4 billion in cash and $12 billion in Facebook stock. The deal valued WhatsApp at roughly $40 per user.

WhatsApp has grown to over 2 billion users globally and remains one of the world's most widely used communications platforms.

headline: Microsoft Acquires Activision Blizzard for $68.7 Billion
subheadline: Gaming mega-deal cleared after 20-month regulatory battle across three continents
body:
Microsoft completed its $68.7 billion all-cash acquisition of Activision Blizzard, the largest gaming deal in history. The deal brought Call of Duty, World of Warcraft, Candy Crush, and Diablo under Xbox Game Studios.

The acquisition faced fierce opposition from regulators worldwide. The FTC sought to block the deal but failed. The UK CMA cleared a restructured version requiring Microsoft to divest cloud gaming rights to Ubisoft.

The deal positions Microsoft to accelerate Game Pass and compete more aggressively in mobile gaming.

headline: Broadcom Acquires VMware for $61 Billion
subheadline: Chip and software giant completes one of the largest technology acquisitions in history
body:
Broadcom completed its $61 billion acquisition of VMware following nearly 18 months of regulatory review across the US, EU, UK, China, and other jurisdictions.

Broadcom financed the cash portion with approximately $32 billion in debt. VMware shareholders received $142.50 per share in cash or 0.2520 Broadcom shares.

CEO Hock Tan restructured VMware's business model post-close, transitioning from perpetual licences to subscriptions.

headline: Amazon Acquires MGM for $8.45 Billion
subheadline: E-commerce giant secures iconic Hollywood studio and James Bond franchise
body:
Amazon completed its $8.45 billion acquisition of MGM Holdings, bringing the legendary studio with its 4,000-film library into the Amazon Prime Video ecosystem.

MGM's library includes James Bond, Rocky, Creed, and The Handmaid's Tale. Amazon Studios gains decades of intellectual property.

The FTC allowed the deal to proceed without conditions. Amazon has rebranded the Epix channel as MGM+ to compete with Netflix and Disney+.

headline: S&P Global Acquires IHS Markit for $44 Billion
subheadline: Financial data giants combine to challenge Bloomberg's market dominance
body:
S&P Global completed its $44 billion all-stock merger with IHS Markit, creating one of the world's most comprehensive financial data and analytics providers.

The deal required significant divestitures including the sale of OPIS commodity pricing business to News Corp for $1.15 billion.

The combined company serves financial institutions, corporations, and governments across 100+ countries.

headline: AstraZeneca Acquires Alexion Pharmaceuticals for $39 Billion
subheadline: British pharma enters rare disease market with landmark acquisition
body:
AstraZeneca completed its $39 billion acquisition of Alexion Pharmaceuticals, its largest ever acquisition and entry into rare disease therapeutics.

Alexion's flagship drugs Soliris and Ultomiris treat rare blood and neurological disorders generating multi-billion dollar annual revenues.

Regulatory approvals were obtained across the US, EU, and multiple other jurisdictions with minimal conditions.

headline: Salesforce Acquires Slack for $27.7 Billion
subheadline: CRM giant bets on workplace messaging to challenge Microsoft Teams
body:
Salesforce completed its $27.7 billion acquisition of Slack, the largest acquisition in Salesforce history at the time.

The deal was driven by Salesforce's ambition to compete with Microsoft Teams in enterprise collaboration. Slack's platform complemented Salesforce's CRM products.

Slack has since been integrated into Salesforce's Customer 360 platform.

headline: ExxonMobil Acquires Pioneer Natural Resources for $60 Billion
subheadline: Largest US oil deal in decades doubles Exxon's Permian Basin footprint
body:
ExxonMobil completed its $60 billion all-stock acquisition of Pioneer Natural Resources, the largest oil deal in the United States in decades.

The deal doubled ExxonMobil's Permian Basin production capacity, adding Pioneer's approximately 850,000 net acres in the Midland Basin.

The FTC cleared the deal but barred Pioneer's former CEO Scott Sheffield from joining ExxonMobil's board.

headline: CVS Health Acquires Aetna for $69 Billion
subheadline: Pharmacy chain and health insurer unite to transform American healthcare delivery
body:
CVS Health completed its $69 billion acquisition of Aetna, one of the largest healthcare deals in US history, combining the largest US pharmacy chain with the third-largest health insurer.

The strategic vision centred on transforming CVS locations into community health hubs offering primary care and chronic disease management.

The DOJ approved the deal subject to Aetna divesting its Medicare Part D prescription drug plan business.

headline: Bristol-Myers Squibb Acquires Celgene for $74 Billion
subheadline: Pharma giant secures blockbuster Revlimid in one of history's largest drug deals
body:
Bristol-Myers Squibb completed its $74 billion acquisition of Celgene, one of the largest pharmaceutical transactions in history giving BMS ownership of Revlimid, one of the world's best-selling cancer drugs.

Regulators required divestitures of Celgene's psoriasis drug Otezla to Amgen for $13.4 billion as a condition of approval.

The strategic combination has since proven successful with BMS building one of the industry's strongest oncology portfolios.

headline: AbbVie Acquires Allergan for $63 Billion
subheadline: Botox maker joins AbbVie as Humira biosimilar competition looms
body:
AbbVie completed its $63 billion acquisition of Allergan, combining AbbVie's immunology portfolio with Allergan's aesthetics, neuroscience, and eye care businesses.

The acquisition was driven by AbbVie's need to diversify revenue ahead of biosimilar competition for Humira. Allergan's Botox franchise provided a high-margin revenue stream.

The FTC required AbbVie to divest several drugs as conditions of approval.

headline: Pfizer Acquires Seagen for $43 Billion
subheadline: Pharma giant secures antibody-drug conjugate oncology platform
body:
Pfizer completed its $43 billion all-cash acquisition of Seagen, a pioneer in antibody-drug conjugate cancer therapies.

Seagen's ADC technology links cancer-targeting antibodies to cytotoxic payloads enabling more precise delivery of chemotherapy directly to tumour cells.

FTC approval came in December 2023 after Pfizer agreed to license certain Seagen assets related to bladder cancer treatments.

headline: Elon Musk Acquires Twitter for $44 Billion
subheadline: World's richest man takes social media platform private in year's most chaotic deal
body:
Elon Musk completed his $44 billion acquisition of Twitter at $54.20 per share. Musk originally agreed in April 2022, then attempted to withdraw in July, before being forced to proceed after Twitter sued for breach of contract.

Musk financed the transaction with $13 billion in bank debt, $7.1 billion from co-investors, and approximately $24 billion from his own wealth.

Musk rebranded the platform as X and made sweeping changes to content moderation and the verification system.

headline: Amazon Acquires Whole Foods for $13.7 Billion
subheadline: E-commerce giant makes dramatic entry into physical grocery retail
body:
Amazon completed its $13.7 billion all-cash acquisition of Whole Foods Market, marking Amazon's most significant expansion into physical retail.

The deal gave Amazon 470 Whole Foods stores across the US, Canada, and UK. Amazon immediately lowered prices at Whole Foods on the day of closing.

The acquisition accelerated Amazon's grocery ambitions and triggered development of Amazon Fresh physical stores.

headline: Bayer Acquires Monsanto for $63 Billion
subheadline: German pharma giant creates global agriculture champion in controversial deal
body:
Bayer AG completed its $63 billion all-cash acquisition of Monsanto, creating the world's largest integrated pesticides and seeds company. The deal required Bayer to divest approximately $9 billion in assets.

Bayer has since faced tens of billions in legal liability related to Roundup's alleged carcinogenicity.

As a condition of closing, Bayer retired the Monsanto brand entirely.

headline: Capital One Acquires Discover Financial for $35.3 Billion
subheadline: Credit card mega-merger creates largest US credit card company by loan volume
body:
Capital One Financial completed its $35.3 billion acquisition of Discover Financial Services, creating the largest US credit card company by loan volume after receiving regulatory approvals.

Capital One gains Discover's payment network, transforming it from a card issuer into a vertically integrated payments company.

Regulators required Capital One to maintain Discover's acceptance network and certain consumer benefits as conditions of approval.

headline: Mars Acquires Kellanova for $35.9 Billion
subheadline: Candy giant adds Pringles, Cheez-It and Pop-Tarts in biggest food deal in years
body:
Mars announced its $35.9 billion all-cash acquisition of Kellanova, adding Pringles, Cheez-It, Pop-Tarts, and Eggo waffles to its portfolio of M&Ms, Snickers, and Wrigley brands.

Mars paid $83.50 per Kellanova share, a 33% premium, financed entirely with cash.

The deal received regulatory approval in the US, EU, and other jurisdictions.

headline: Google Agrees to Acquire Cybersecurity Startup Wiz for $23 Billion
subheadline: Alphabet's largest ever acquisition bets on cloud security dominance
body:
Google agreed to acquire cybersecurity company Wiz for $23 billion, Alphabet's largest ever acquisition. Wiz had rejected an earlier approach before ultimately agreeing.

Wiz provides cloud security software helping organisations identify vulnerabilities across multi-cloud environments, reaching $500M ARR faster than any software company.

The acquisition faces regulatory review from the DOJ and international antitrust authorities.

headline: Synopsys Completes $35 Billion Acquisition of Ansys
subheadline: EDA and simulation software combine to power next-generation AI chip design
body:
Synopsys completed its $35 billion acquisition of Ansys following regulatory approvals across the US, EU, UK, South Korea, and Taiwan.

The combination integrates Synopsys's EDA tools with Ansys's simulation software for next-generation AI chip development.

The deal required Synopsys to divest certain assets to satisfy competition regulators.

headline: Biden Blocks Nippon Steel's $14.9 Billion Bid for US Steel
subheadline: National security review kills Japanese steelmaker's landmark acquisition
body:
President Biden formally blocked Nippon Steel's proposed $14.9 billion acquisition of US Steel on national security grounds following a CFIUS review.

Nippon Steel had offered $55 per share, a 142% premium. Both President Biden and President-elect Trump publicly opposed the deal.

US Steel and Nippon Steel sued the Biden administration arguing the CFIUS process was politically motivated.

headline: OpenAI Raises $6.6 Billion at $157 Billion Valuation
subheadline: ChatGPT maker secures largest venture funding round in history
body:
OpenAI closed a $6.6 billion funding round led by Thrive Capital valuing the company at $157 billion post-money, the largest venture capital funding round in history.

Investors including Microsoft, Nvidia, SoftBank, and Khosla Ventures participated in the oversubscribed round.

The fundraise came as OpenAI was generating approximately $3.7 billion in annualised revenue.

headline: KKR Acquires RJR Nabisco for $31 Billion
subheadline: Barbarians at the Gate — the leveraged buyout that defined a generation
body:
Kohlberg Kravis Roberts completed its $31 billion leveraged buyout of RJR Nabisco, the largest LBO in history at the time. The deal became the subject of the book and film Barbarians at the Gate.

KKR prevailed with a bid of $109 per share, financed almost entirely with junk bonds arranged by Drexel Burnham Lambert.

The deal defined the leveraged buyout era of the 1980s and remains a landmark case study in corporate finance.

headline: AMD Acquires Xilinx for $49 Billion
subheadline: Chip maker gains FPGA leadership to challenge Intel across data centre markets
body:
AMD completed its $49 billion all-stock acquisition of Xilinx, the world's largest maker of FPGAs, the largest acquisition in AMD's history.

The deal gave AMD a comprehensive data centre portfolio combining CPUs, GPUs, and adaptive computing chips.

China's SAMR approved the deal in January 2022 as the final regulatory hurdle.

headline: J&J Spins Off Consumer Health as Kenvue in $41 Billion IPO
subheadline: Tylenol, Listerine, and Band-Aid brands go public as standalone company
body:
Johnson & Johnson completed the IPO of its consumer health division as Kenvue on the NYSE, raising approximately $3.8 billion at an implied valuation of $41 billion.

Kenvue's portfolio includes Tylenol, Motrin, Neutrogena, Aveeno, Listerine, and Band-Aid.

J&J distributed its remaining 89.6% stake to shareholders through an exchange offer completed in August 2023.

headline: BlackRock Acquires Barclays Global Investors for $13.5 Billion
subheadline: Deal creates world's largest asset manager with $3.2 trillion under management
body:
BlackRock completed its $13.5 billion acquisition of Barclays Global Investors, creating the world's largest asset management firm with $3.2 trillion in AUM.

The deal brought iShares, the world's largest ETF platform, under BlackRock's umbrella.

CEO Larry Fink used the acquisition to transform BlackRock into the most influential asset manager globally with AUM now exceeding $10 trillion.

headline: T-Mobile Merges with Sprint in $26 Billion Deal
subheadline: Wireless merger creates stronger third competitor to AT&T and Verizon
body:
T-Mobile completed its merger with Sprint creating a combined company with approximately 100 million customers.

The DOJ approved the deal subject to divestiture of Sprint's prepaid brands to Dish Network, which agreed to build a new fourth wireless network.

The combined company has aggressively rolled out 5G coverage using Sprint's mid-band spectrum holdings.

headline: Berkshire Hathaway Acquires BNSF Railway for $44 Billion
subheadline: Warren Buffett's biggest ever bet on the economic future of America
body:
Berkshire Hathaway completed its acquisition of Burlington Northern Santa Fe for approximately $44 billion. Warren Buffett described it as an all-in wager on the economic future of the United States.

Berkshire paid $100 per share in cash and stock for the remaining 78% it did not already own, a 31% premium.

BNSF operates one of North America's largest freight rail networks spanning 32,500 miles across 28 states.

headline: SoftBank Acquires Arm Holdings for $32 Billion
subheadline: Japanese tech investor bets on IoT future with chip architecture pioneer
body:
SoftBank Group completed its £24.3 billion acquisition of Arm Holdings in September 2016, the largest ever acquisition of a European technology company at the time.

SoftBank founder Masayoshi Son described Arm as central to his vision of the IoT revolution. The deal was completed entirely in cash.

SoftBank later attempted to sell Arm to Nvidia for $40 billion but the deal collapsed. SoftBank took Arm public on Nasdaq in September 2023.

headline: Bank of America Acquires Merrill Lynch for $50 Billion
subheadline: Crisis-era deal saves Merrill from collapse but nearly sinks Bank of America
body:
Bank of America completed its acquisition of Merrill Lynch for approximately $50 billion, hastily arranged over a single weekend in September 2008 as Lehman Brothers collapsed.

The deal was encouraged by US Treasury Secretary Paulson and Fed Chairman Bernanke. Merrill posted $15 billion in losses in Q4 2008 shocking Bank of America shareholders.

Bank of America required a $20 billion government bailout and paid $17 billion in legal settlements related to Merrill's mortgage securities.

headline: Dow Chemical and DuPont Merge in $130 Billion Deal
subheadline: Two of America's oldest industrial giants combine then split into three companies
body:
Dow Chemical and DuPont completed their merger of equals creating DowDuPont with a combined market capitalisation of approximately $130 billion.

The deal was structured with the explicit intention of subsequently splitting into three independent companies focused on agriculture, materials science, and specialty products.

All three separations were completed by 2019 creating Corteva, Dow Inc., and DuPont de Nemours.

headline: London Stock Exchange Acquires Refinitiv for $27 Billion
subheadline: Historic exchange transformed into global financial data powerhouse
body:
London Stock Exchange Group completed its $27 billion acquisition of Refinitiv, transforming LSEG into a global financial markets infrastructure and data company.

Refinitiv owns the Eikon financial terminal, Tradeweb bond trading platform, and FXall foreign exchange platform.

Regulatory conditions required LSEG to divest its Borsa Italiana exchange to Euronext for approximately €4.3 billion.

headline: Prologis Acquires Duke Realty for $26 Billion
subheadline: Industrial REIT mega-merger creates dominant US logistics property platform
body:
Prologis completed its $26 billion all-stock acquisition of Duke Realty, the largest REIT acquisition in history at the time with 159 million square feet of logistics facilities.

The deal gave Prologis an unassailable position as the world's largest industrial REIT driven by e-commerce demand.

Prologis paid 0.466 shares per Duke share at a premium of approximately 29%.

headline: Nvidia Acquires Mellanox Technologies for $6.9 Billion
subheadline: GPU maker secures high-speed networking critical for AI supercomputing clusters
body:
Nvidia completed its $6.9 billion all-cash acquisition of Mellanox Technologies, securing InfiniBand and Ethernet networking equipment essential for connecting GPUs in AI training clusters.

The acquisition proved transformative as AI demand exploded. Mellanox's networking is central to Nvidia's DGX AI supercomputer systems.

China's SAMR approved the deal in April 2020 as the final regulatory hurdle.

headline: Microsoft Acquires Nuance Communications for $19.7 Billion
subheadline: Healthcare AI deal bolsters Microsoft's clinical documentation and enterprise AI
body:
Microsoft completed its $19.7 billion acquisition of Nuance Communications, the leading provider of AI-powered clinical documentation software used by healthcare providers across the US.

The deal strengthened Microsoft's healthcare cloud offerings and provided a major enterprise AI product.

Microsoft has since integrated Nuance's Dragon Copilot into its Microsoft 365 Copilot suite for healthcare organisations.

headline: ChemChina Acquires Syngenta for $43 Billion
subheadline: Largest ever Chinese overseas acquisition transforms global agriculture
body:
China National Chemical Corporation completed its $43 billion acquisition of Swiss agrochemicals giant Syngenta, the largest ever overseas acquisition by a Chinese company.

The deal gave China access to Syngenta's world-leading pesticide and seed technology to improve agricultural productivity.

The transaction required regulatory approval from more than 20 countries.

headline: ADNOC Acquires German Chemicals Giant Covestro for $16.1 Billion
subheadline: Abu Dhabi's energy giant secures European specialty materials leader
body:
ADNOC completed its acquisition of Covestro for €14.7 billion, one of the largest Middle Eastern acquisitions of a European industrial company.

ADNOC was attracted by Covestro's sustainable materials technology used in electric vehicles, building insulation, and medical devices.

The acquisition required regulatory approval across the EU and multiple other jurisdictions.

headline: Diamondback Energy Acquires Endeavor Energy for $26 Billion
subheadline: Permian Basin consolidation continues with largest-ever private US oil acquisition
body:
Diamondback Energy completed its $26 billion acquisition of Endeavor Energy Resources, the largest acquisition of a private US oil company in history.

Endeavor produced approximately 350,000 barrels of oil equivalent per day from prime Midland Basin acreage.

The deal made Diamondback one of the largest pure-play Permian Basin operators.

headline: Amazon Invests $4 Billion in Anthropic at $18 Billion Valuation
subheadline: Claude AI maker secures landmark cloud deal with world's largest cloud provider
body:
Anthropic completed a $4 billion strategic investment from Amazon Web Services, securing a deep cloud partnership under which Anthropic's Claude AI models are available through AWS Bedrock.

The investment required Anthropic to use AWS as its primary cloud provider for training and deploying AI models.

Google had previously invested in Anthropic and subsequently committed up to $2 billion.

headline: Glaxo Wellcome and SmithKline Beecham Merge for $76 Billion
subheadline: Pharmaceutical giants combine to create world's largest drug company
body:
Glaxo Wellcome and SmithKline Beecham completed their merger creating GlaxoSmithKline, the world's largest pharmaceutical company with a 7% share of the global prescription drug market.

The deal was the second attempt at a merger, the first having collapsed in 1998. The combined entity brought together blockbusters including Paxil, Augmentin, and Zantac.

GlaxoSmithKline spun off its consumer healthcare division Haleon in 2022 in one of the largest demergers in British corporate history.

headline: London Stock Exchange Acquires Refinitiv for $27 Billion
subheadline: Historic exchange transformed into global financial data powerhouse
body:
London Stock Exchange Group completed its $27 billion acquisition of Refinitiv, transforming LSEG from a traditional exchange into a global financial markets infrastructure and data company.

Refinitiv owns the Eikon terminal, Tradeweb bond trading platform, and FXall foreign exchange platform.

Regulatory conditions required LSEG to divest Borsa Italiana to Euronext for approximately €4.3 billion.
